title: S-Pers branch-state persistence contract
description: Specifies exactly which branch-state coordinates and typed data persist across a transition B -> B'. The record operationalizes the S-Pers persistence lemma schema in the structural exhaustion framework (branch_closure_methodology_extended.tex, "Lemma schemas").

schema.description: Required contract discriminator; it must be the literal `S-Pers`.

fromStateRef.description: Required nonempty stable identifier of the source branch state before the transition.

toStateRef.description: Required nonempty stable identifier of the destination branch state after the transition.

preserved.description: Required structured inventory of everything carried from the source state into the destination state; all five categories must be present even when a category's list is empty.

hypotheses.description: Required list of baseline assumptions from H_0 still true after the transition, including their carrier objects, tactic scope, and any proof-language or admission assumptions that remain in force.

items.description: One preserved hypothesis, stated precisely enough to identify its scope in the destination state.

exclusions.description: Required list of already closed exits from E that remain unavailable, such as target certificates, compression exits, routed defects with consumers, impossible ledgers/budgets, or finite-table impossibility rows.

items.description: One preserved exclusion, including the result or contradiction that established it.

invariants.description: Required list of structural or quantitative invariants from I retained by the transition, including minimality consequences, forbidden-response statements, density or rank bounds, monotonicity facts, capacity estimates, exact-type equalities, ledgers, budgets, and response data.

items.description: One invariant proved to hold in both the source and destination states.

residualConditions.description: Required list of residual predicates that continue to define the surviving subcase, with their exact inequalities or structural restrictions.

items.description: One carried residual condition from R, stated as a predicate, comparison, trigger condition, or typed survivor-class restriction for the destination branch state.

vocabularyEntries.description: Required list of typed objects and notation from V still available downstream; entries should name their defining S-Def/S-Det/S-Rout records so symbols, payload handles, budgets, ledgers, exact types, or certificate handles are not used after their scope has ended.

items.description: One preserved typed symbol, construction, payload handle, or evidence reference.

strengthenedInvariants.description: Optional list of invariants that become strictly stronger in the destination state, such as a sharper ledger balance, budget slack, capacity estimate, monotonicity fact, exact-type equality, rank bound, or forbidden-response statement recorded in I.

items.description: One newly strengthened invariant and the exact improvement over its source-state version.

newlyAbsentExits.description: Optional list of branch exits newly inserted into E by the transition proof, such as an alternative closed by C1-C5 or a payload route proved impossible for this residual.

items.description: One newly excluded exit together with its proof-flow or labelled-result reference.

evidence.description: Required nonempty list of sources proving every claimed preservation, strengthening, and newly absent exit.

items.description: One evidence record supporting the transition's persistence claims.

kind.description: Required evidence category from the fixed evidence-kind alphabet.

reference.description: Required nonempty locator for the relevant persistence argument, S-Pers record, transition proof, route check, ledger update, budget update, branch-tree row, or audit entry.

hash.description: Optional integrity hash for a version-sensitive table, program, or formal proof artifact.

notes.description: Optional note mapping the cited evidence to particular preserved or strengthened entries.
